title: Iodine Calculator: Pregnancy & Lactation | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Much Iodine Do You Really Need?
description: Calculate your daily iodine requirements for pregnancy, breastfeeding, and general health. Find iodine-rich foods, understand RDA by life stage.
seoKeywords: how much iodine do I need
intro: **Iodine** is essential for thyroid hormone production (T3 and T4) and fetal brain development. Iodine deficiency during pregnancy is the **#1 preventable cause of intellectual disability** worldwide. The RDA is **150 mcg/day for adults**, increasing to **220 mcg during pregnancy** and **290 mcg while breastfeeding** (WHO). Many countries mandate iodized salt to prevent deficiency, but it persists among those using non-iodized salt (Himalayan, sea salt) or limiting salt intake. This calculator returns your daily iodine target based on your life stage and identifies the best sources: iodized salt (1g = 20–40 mcg), seafood (~50 mcg/100g), seaweed (variable), and dairy (100 mcg/glass). Caution: excess iodine (>1,100 mcg/day) from seaweed can trigger thyroiditis.
keyTakeaway: Iodine: **150 mcg daily for adults / 220 mcg for pregnancy / 290 mcg for breastfeeding**. Best sources: iodized salt, seafood, dairy.
useCases: Vegetarian or vegan diet (no fish or dairy)

example.title: Example: Pregnant Woman
steps: **Sources**: 1.5g iodized salt (45 mcg) + 100g hake (50 mcg) + 1 glass milk (100 mcg) = 195 mcg + prenatal supplement 150 mcg.
example.result: You need **220 mcg/day**. All prenatal supplements contain iodine (typically 150 mcg). Add seafood twice weekly.

explanation:
## What is Iodine

A mineral essential for thyroid hormone production and neurological health.

## Daily Recommended Intake (RDA)

| Life Stage | mcg/day |
|---|---|
| Infant 0–6 months | 110 |
| Infant 7–12 months | 130 |
| Children 1–8 years | 90 |
| Children 9–13 years | 120 |
| Adolescents/Adults | 150 |
| Pregnancy | 220 |
| Breastfeeding | 290 |

## Food Sources (mcg per 100g)

| Food | Iodine |
|---|---|
| Kelp seaweed | 2000–40000 ⚠️ |
| Cod | 170 |
| Iodized salt (1g) | 20–40 |
| Hake | 50 |
| Salmon | 40 |
| Dairy products | 40–80 |
| Egg | 25 |
| Yogurt | 30 |

## Iodized Salt Standards

Many countries mandate iodized salt to prevent deficiency. For example, Argentina's Law 17,259 (1967) requires table salt to contain iodine (15–40 mg/kg). Switching to non-iodized varieties (Himalayan, sea salt) eliminates this important dietary source.

## Iodine Deficiency Consequences

- **Pregnancy**: Fetal cognitive delay, cretinism
- **Adults**: Goiter, hypothyroidism
- **Children**: Cognitive impairment

## Excess Iodine

- **Upper Limit (UL) is 1,100 mcg/day** for adults
- Seaweed can contain 2,000+ mcg per serving
- Excess iodine can trigger Hashimoto's thyroiditis in susceptible individuals

## Common Mistakes

1. Using non-iodized salt (pink, sea salt) without supplementation
2. Vegans relying on seaweed without medical supervision
3. Consuming excessive seaweed
4. Pregnancy without prenatal supplementation

## Related Calculators

- [Selenium](/calculator-daily-selenium): Iodine-to-selenium balance
- [Vegan Macros](/calculator-vegan-macros-diet): Complete nutrient planning
- [Sodium](/calculator-daily-sodium): Manage sodium intake strategically

Consult an endocrinologist if you have thyroid concerns.

faq.q: How much iodine do I need during pregnancy?
faq.a: 220 mcg/day according to WHO and NIH guidelines.

faq.q: Does pink Himalayan salt contain iodine?
faq.a: No. Most non-iodized salts lack iodine, potentially causing deficiency.

faq.q: Is seaweed a good iodine source?
faq.a: Yes, in moderation. Kelp can be extremely high (2,000+ mcg/serving), so be cautious.
Most do—typically around 150 mcg. Check your label to confirm.

faq.q: Can too much iodine be harmful?
faq.a: Yes. The upper limit is 1,100 mcg/day. Excess iodine can trigger thyroiditis.

faq.q: How do vegans and vegetarians get enough iodine?
faq.a: Use iodized salt, consume moderate seaweed (like nori), or take a 150 mcg supplement.

faq.q: Can iodine deficiency affect my baby?
faq.a: Yes. Severe deficiency during pregnancy can cause intellectual disability and cretinism.

faq.q: What are the signs of iodine deficiency?
faq.a: Goiter (enlarged thyroid), fatigue, weight gain, difficulty concentrating, and cognitive issues.
